messages.content:
CVE-2021-34897 is a high-severity vulnerability affecting Bentley View version 10.15.0.75. It enables remote code execution by exploiting a flaw in the parsing of DGN files, where crafted data can lead to a buffer overflow. Successful exploitation requires user interaction, such as opening a malicious file or visiting a harmful webpage.

This vulnerability is categorized under CWE-787, which pertains to improper buffer boundaries. An attacker can use this flaw to execute code in the context of the current process, potentially leading to a full system compromise. The vulnerability has been tracked as ZDI-CAN-14864 and is associated with MITRE ATT&CK technique T1203 (Exploitation for Client Execution).

Affected products include Bentley View and MicroStation versions prior to 10.16.02. Users are advised to update to the latest versions to mitigate the risk. Implementing mitigation strategies M1050, M1051, and M1048 can further enhance defense against exploitation attempts.
